text:
I dinged this one star because the programming through the controller just doesnвҖҷt work that well IF you run a fan and humidifier under the same application. What happens, the fan and humidifier start working against each other, so if the temp target is 80, yet humidity is 60, once the 80 temp is surpassed, the fan turns on, which of course, drops the humidity, regardless of whether itвҖҷs reached 60. The way to fix this to run separate program activity simultaneously, on separate ports. So youвҖҷll have the fan running independently of the humidifier. Kind of a pain, but pretty easy once you figure it out. Also, it WILL NOT let you run target levels on two devices using one application, you have to enter two separate applications for each device.
Hope this helps someone. ACInfinity makes great stuff with great service, too.

text: I like this humidifier. It's not perfect but I'm happy with it and I would recommend it to others. I think the good outweighs the bad. Here is the good and bad:The good:-- it is extremely quiet even on the highest level. Some humidifiers make dribbling/gurgling sounds. This does not.-- the app is excellent. I had low expectations but it ended up being one of my favorite aspects of this product. Connects easily and I can adjust the humidifier from anywhere in the house. It also lets you be more specific on the humidity output level, whereas the machine itself only lets you select low (level 1) or high (level 8)-- the night light is also excellent if you need one. It can change to many different colors and brightnesses. It has a few preprogrammed color change cycles if you want.-- it shuts off when out of water. Not all humidifiers do this.-- the cavity of the water tank is big enough to fit your hand into for cleaning (but see below)The bad (all cleaning related):-- The water tank is large enough to fit your hand for cleaning, which is great. But there are SO many nooks and crannies that your hand can't get to. It's design has places where there are like 2mm gaps between parts that no sponge or brush or tool is ever going to fit in to. So while your hand does fit into the tank it doesn't mean you can actually clean every bit of the tank.-- The instruction manual inexplicably says to clean only by drying with a towel and to never use a detergent in it. This is insane. You have to sanitize a humidifier every few days to keep mold out and towel drying isn't going to cut it. So I use hydrogen peroxide because I figure it's pretty delicate unlike bleach, which I would normally use. You will get mold if you don't sanitize. Water and mold are best buddies. This is one of those cases where you just plain shouldn't follow what the manual says.-- There are two tanks to clean, a large upper water reservoir and a lower tank with the mechanics in it. The lower tank is hard to clean without getting water into the power cord hole and the night light isn't waterproof and you can't get that part wet. It's not impossible to clean it's just inconvenient.I've attached some pictures of the inside of the humidifier and the various parts that need to be cleaned.Like I said at the start, it's a great product and I'm happy with it. I just wish they would have thought more about how to actually clean every single part.

One small part of the leg was resting on our living room rug and it caused the swinging to stop way earlier than when it was flat on the hard floor. The unit feels sturdy and itвҖҷs very attractive.
